questions.question: What does Box<T> provide?
questions.explanation: Box<T> puts data on the heap with single ownership. Essential for recursive types.

questions.question: Rc<T> is safe to use across multiple threads.
questions.explanation: Rc uses non-atomic reference counting — not thread-safe. Use Arc<T> for multithreaded shared ownership.

questions.question: Create a Box to enable a recursive type:
questions.explanation: Box gives the recursive List a known size (one pointer) so the compiler accepts it.

questions.question: What does Arc<T> stand for?
questions.explanation: Arc = Atomically Reference Counted. Thread-safe version of Rc with atomic operations.

questions.question: Share ownership with Rc by cloning the handle:
questions.explanation: Rc::clone increments the reference count without deep-copying the data.

questions.question: The Deref trait lets smart pointers be used like regular references.
questions.explanation: Deref enables deref coercion: &Box<T> automatically becomes &T.
